hikes.name: Pico Paraná Trail
hikes.title: Challenging but rewarding summit!
hikes.description: An intense hike to the highest peak in Southern Brazil. The views above the clouds are absolutely unforgettable, but it requires good physical preparation.

hikes.name: Salto São Francisco
hikes.title: Breathtakingly massive waterfall
hikes.description: The trail leads to an incredible lookout point of one of the largest waterfalls in the region. The mist from the falls feels amazing after the walk.

hikes.name: Canyon Guartelá Path
hikes.title: Stunning canyon vistas
hikes.description: An easy-to-moderate trail offering expansive views of the world's sixth-longest canyon. The rock formations and native vegetation are fascinating.

hikes.name: Morro do Canal
hikes.title: Perfect quick weekend hike
hikes.description: A shorter, steep climb with iron ladders near the top. Great panoramic views of the coastal mountains and Curitiba on a clear day.

hikes.name: Salto dos Macacos
hikes.title: A tough trek to paradise
hikes.description: Following old train tracks and dense Atlantic forest trails leads to a gorgeous natural pool at the base of two massive waterfalls. Perfect for a swim.

hikes.name: Caminho do Itupava
hikes.title: Way too dangerous and poorly maintained
hikes.description: The historic stones are completely covered in slick moss and multiple sections of the trail are washed out. Skip this until repairs are done.

hikes.name: Morro Araçatuba
hikes.title: Beautiful fields and windy peaks
hikes.description: A steady climb through open mountain grasslands. The summit offers a 360-degree view of the surrounding valleys and can get incredibly windy.

hikes.name: Buraco do Padre
hikes.title: A hidden waterfall inside a cave!
hikes.description: An extremely accessible and easy walk through a gorge leading inside a giant vertical cave where a waterfall cascades down. Magical place.

hikes.name: Recanto dos Papagaios Trail
hikes.title: Relaxing riverside walk
hikes.description: A peaceful trail running alongside the river. Great for families and those looking for a light stroll surrounded by beautiful pine trees.

hikes.name: Pico Caratuva Trail
hikes.title: Tough climb, incredible landscape
hikes.description: The second highest peak in the south. The trail is steep and muddy through the forest, but opening up to the peak gives a stunning view of Pico Paraná.

hikes.name: Salto Pito Trail
hikes.title: Nice falls, but completely overcrowded
hikes.description: The waterfall itself is massive, but the trail is packed shoulder-to-shoulder on weekends and littered with plastic wrappers. Left disappointed.

hikes.name: Anhangava Mountain
hikes.title: The cradle of local climbing
hikes.description: Very popular trail for hiking and rock climbing. A relatively quick ascent with some rocky scrambles near the top. Great for watching the sunrise.

hikes.name: Salto São João
hikes.title: A stunning drop in the forest
hikes.description: The trail takes you to an observation deck looking directly at a massive 84-meter high waterfall surrounded by lush green canyon walls.

hikes.name: Morro do Marumbi (Olimpo)
hikes.title: Completely missing trail markers
hikes.description: Attempted this trail but had to turn around because the path is overgrown and the markers are missing. Extremely easy to get lost.

hikes.name: Parque Das Monções Trail
hikes.title: Underwhelming and full of mosquitoes
hikes.description: The historical context is neat, but the trail itself is very short, uninspired, and right next to heavily polluted river water. Bring heavy bug spray.
